title: rubus lacustris
intro: Rubus lacustris, also known as the lake raspberry or lake thimbleberry, is a species of thorny shrub in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus lacustris include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antioxidant. Rubus lacustris can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, with the dried leaves and roots being commonly used in herbal preparations.
